prompts:
from competition fish

Two prompts for CodeFish:

Prompt 8 — Wire CompetitionCloud API + fix Netrewards parser:

Update ~/compfish/compfish_scraper.py with these two fixes:

1. REPLACE scrape_competitioncloud() entirely. Use their AJAX endpoint:

POST https://www.competitioncloud.com.au/Home/GetCompetitionPartial

Hit it with requests.post() (no Playwright needed). It returns HTML fragments with competition cards. Parse the HTML response with BeautifulSoup to extract titles, prize values, entry URLs, and closing dates. Try these POST params first:
- Empty body
- {"pageNumber": 1}
- {"page": 1}

Test which one returns data. Keep paginating until CheckMoreCompetitionAvailableOrNot returns false.

Feed all results into add_competition() with source="competitioncloud.com.au".

2. FIX scrape_netrewards() — the comp data is in the page but the selector was wrong. Instead of looking for "competitions-prize" in href, extract competition info from:
- Image filenames (e.g., Great-Southern-Bank-Win-50K-Cash.webp)
- The surrounding link/text near each image
- Use Playwright since the page renders fine, just fix the selector logic

After updating, run a full scrape:
cd ~/compfish && ./venv/bin/python3 compfish_daemon.py scrape

Report the count per source.

Prompt 9 — Build the hop-through entry recon:

The top 5 comps in ~/compfish/compfish.db all point to aussiecomps listing pages, not the actual entry forms. The real entry form is one click deeper.

Write a new script: ~/compfish/compfish_recon.py

It should:
1. Load top 20 enterable comps from the database (use get_enterable_comps(20))
2. For each comp, use Playwright to:
   a. Navigate to the entry_url (aussiecomps listing page)
   b. Find the external link to the actual competition (look for "Enter", "Enter Now", "Visit Site", or outbound links that aren't aussiecomps.com)
   c. Follow that link to the real competition page
   d. Scan the real page for:
      - Form fields (input, select, textarea)
      - CAPTCHA presence (recaptcha, hcaptcha, turnstile)
      - Platform (Gleam, ShortStack, Woobox, Typeform, Google Forms, direct site form)
      - Social login requirements
   e. Take a screenshot of the real entry page
   f. Save results to a new table in compfish.db:

      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entry_recon (
          comp_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
          real_entry_url TEXT,
          platform TEXT,
          form_fields TEXT,  -- JSON list of field names/types
          has_captcha INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
          needs_social INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
          difficulty TEXT,  -- easy/medium/hard/manual
          screenshot_path TEXT,
          recon_date TEXT
      )

3. Print a summary: how many are easy (simple form, no captcha), medium (captcha but automatable), hard (social login or complex), manual (external platform like Instagram)

Run it on the top 20:
cd ~/compfish && ./venv/bin/python3 compfish_recon.py

Show me the difficulty breakdown.

Prompt 8 unlocks the other scrapers. Prompt 9 tells us exactly which comps Fish can auto-enter. Both can run in parallel again.
